title: How This Emerging Tick-borne Virus Escapes Your Immune System
gist: A 2026 study in ACS Infectious Diseases finds that Pacific Coast tick-borne orthonairoviruses use an immune-suppression mechanism compatible with the human immune system, against the backdrop of an unusually severe 2026 tick season across the Midwest and Northeast.

insights.claim: 2026 has brought an unusually severe tick season, with populations surging in the Midwest and Northeast, raising the risk of tick-borne illness.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: A near-term public-health signal (outdoor recreation, pest-control demand) tied to an unusually bad tick year.
insights.locator: 2026 has seen an unusually rough tick season

insights.claim: The Pacific Coast tick can carry emerging orthonairoviruses, a virus family researchers are newly studying for its ability to evade the human immune system.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Flags an emerging pathogen category worth tracking for public-health and diagnostics relevance.
insights.locator: the Pacific Coast tick can carry

insights.claim: University of California, Riverside researcher Scott Pegan found that a Pacific Coast tick orthonairovirus mechanism for suppressing host immunity is highly compatible with humans, indicating a real health concern from these tick bites.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Suggests this virus family could pose a genuine emerging human health threat worth monitoring.
insights.quote: Our work demonstrates that one of the Pacific Coast tick orthonairovirus mechanisms used to suppress host immunity is highly compatible with humans.
insights.locator: “There is a very real health concern”
